message:
feat(tui): add color blind accessibility modes (deuteranopia, protanopia, tritanopia)

Implements color blind friendly palettes for the TUI dashboard,
ensuring workers with color vision deficiencies can effectively
distinguish status indicators and UI elements.

ColorBlindMode enum:
- None: Default palette (no modification)
- Deuteranopia: Red-green color blindness (most common, ~6% of males)
- Protanopia: Red-green color blindness with red weakness
- Tritanopia: Blue-yellow color blindness (rare)

Integration:
- TuiConfig.color_blind: CLI configuration option
- TuiState.color_blind: Runtime state for rendering
- ValueEnum derive for clap CLI argument parsing
- Serde support for config file persistence

Usage:
  rch status --tui --color-blind=deuteranopia
  rch status --tui --color-blind=protanopia
  rch status --tui --color-blind=tritanopia

Or in config file:
  [tui]
  color_blind = "deuteranopia"

The palettes use distinct hue/saturation combinations that remain
distinguishable under simulated color blindness conditions, following
WCAG 2.1 guidelines for contrast and distinguishability.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
